entries.description: Routed ai-assignment.md analysis (gemini-pro, $0.0162) and saved it to the set folder.

Implemented ai_router/verify_session.py: the Step-6 verify_session CLI (evidence assembly with git status + full diff and default generated-bundle exclusions, verification.md template fill, cross-provider route(task_type=session-verification), raw sN-verification*.md write-before-display, sN-issues*.json envelope on findings, classify_blocking wiring, raw-JSON disposition patch, L-064-7 tier-pin refusal, --dry-run). --help documents the contract.

Layer-1 pytest suite ai_router/tests/test_verify_session.py: 39 tests covering untracked-file visibility, exclusion defaults/overrides, artifact naming across rounds, idempotent field-preserving disposition patch, blocking classification wiring (exit 0/4), dry-run writes-nothing-routes-nothing, tier-pin refusal matrix, route-failure path. Full suite: 2522 passed, 5 skipped.

Dogfood round 1: routed gate REQUIRED (blast-radius, multi-module, breadth); ran this session's own Step 6 via the new CLI. Verifier gpt-5-4 returned ISSUES_FOUND with 2 Major findings ($0.229): (1) round1_verifier_tier read the LAST matching metrics row (would compare the L-064-7 pin against round 2's tier on a round-3 call) - accepted, fixed to first-matching-row + added a regression test; (2) bare 'import verify_session' in the test suite - context-gap vs the documented conftest sys.path convention, but accepted anyway (conftest supports either form; package-qualified import makes the target unambiguous). CLI wrote s1-verification.md, s1-issues.json, and the disposition patch exactly per contract; suite now 40 passed.

Dogfood round 2 ($0.185, gpt-5-4): 1 new Major - the round-1 fix could still fall through to a later round's tier when the round-1 metrics row exists but its tier is unreadable (fail-open contract violated). Accepted, fixed: the first matching row IS round 1 unconditionally; unreadable tier returns None. Added regression test test_unreadable_round1_row_fails_open_not_through. Suite: 41 passed.

Dogfood round 3 ($0.079, gpt-5-4): VERIFIED - the unreadable-round-1-row fix confirmed, no regressions. Dogfood complete: all three Step-6 rounds of this session ran through the new CLI itself. Total routed cost this session ~ $0.51.
Final suite run before close: 2524 passed, 5 skipped (baseline skips), 0 failed.

Disposition validation: legal vocabulary is now (api, manual-via-other-engine, skipped); retired tokens queue (Set 026) and manual (the incident vector, renamed Set 083) rejected with naming messages; dead queue message_ids branch removed; JSON-schema enum + docs/disposition-schema.md brought to parity (L-066-1), stale queue variation section replaced with the routed-gate SKIP exemplar.

check_verification_integrity added to gate_checks.py as the sixth GATE_CHECKS entry: layer 1 rejects illegal method tokens (retired queue/manual get naming messages); layer 2 corroborates any claimed non-null verdict (api: cross-provider session-verification metrics row for this set+session with verifier provider != orchestrator provider from the session-state block, missing identity fails closed, plus an sN-verification*.md artifact; manual-via-other-engine/skipped: budget.yaml must declare threshold_usd 0 and a matching method). Null-verdict SKIP closes (Set 080 S1 shape) stay legal. close_session wiring: --manual-verify records the check as passed-with-note (sanctioned, attested bypass); --force now runs this one check even though it skips the bookkeeping gates (force bypasses gates, not evidence); refusal messages print the exact venv verify_session invocation for this set. Hard-block posture in both interactive and headless modes (deterministic gate path exits 1 regardless of TTY).

42-test matrix in test_verify_session... test_verification_integrity_gate.py incl. the live incident as a regression fixture through the full close_session.run flow (gate_failed, exit 1, headless), --force-does-not-bypass, --manual-verify sanctioned bypass, no-artifact / same-provider / missing-identity / zero-budget cases, and a 13-row _claimed_close_verdict vs resolve_close_verdict parity matrix. Updated 9 existing fixture files that closed with uncorroborated api claims (seeded real evidence rather than weakening the gate). Full Layer-1 suite: 2566 passed, 5 skipped (baseline), 0 failed.

Routed gate: REQUIRED (blast-radius: cross-artifact/shared-schema/wiring; multi-module {ai_router, docs}; breadth 19 files) - per-session cross-provider verification runs via the new verify_session CLI (S1 dogfood continues).

Round 1 (gpt-5-4): ISSUES_FOUND, 3 Major. V1 (Correctness, accepted): _row_provider trusted the metrics rows provider string instead of resolving via the model registry - fixed to registry-only resolution (row provider string deliberately untrusted; unresolvable model/registry fails closed) + 2 regression tests (lying provider string; unresolvable model). V2 (Completeness, accepted): the new test file was untracked so the round-1 diff omitted it (L-064-9 class) - git add -A; round 2 diff carries it. V3 (Completeness, process-order): the session close necessarily happens AFTER Step 6 verification (workflow ordering); the live-gate close dogfood runs immediately after a non-blocking verdict - stated in the round-2 ledger, not fixable within a verification round by construction. Suite after remediation: 2568 passed, 5 skipped.

Round 2 (gpt-5-4): ISSUES_FOUND, 1 Major (accepted): --manual-verify blanket-skipped the whole verification_integrity check, so the incident token could still close under attestation. Fixed: split check_verification_method_vocabulary out of the gate; --manual-verify now bypasses ONLY the evidence-corroboration layer while the vocabulary rule runs on every path (attested close of the exact incident shape now gate_failed; new e2e test pins it, and the sanctioned-bypass test now demonstrates the bypass on a LEGAL token whose evidence would fail). V1/V2 from round 1 remain fixed; V3 remains resolved-by-process-order. Suite: 2569 passed, 5 skipped.

Round 3 (gpt-5-4): ISSUES_FOUND, 1 new Major (accepted): the --force path hard-coded outcome verification.method=skipped even when the disposition carried a legal, gate-corroborated method - the audit surface claimed skipped for a corroborated close. Fixed: force now records the dispositions legal method verbatim (skipped only as the no-usable-disposition fallback); 2 new regression tests (corroborated api under force records api; force without disposition records skipped). All prior ledger items remain RESOLVED; every round has surfaced a NEW distinct defect, all fixed - proceeding to a confirming round 4 per the ledgers no-unresolved-findings state. Suite: 2571 passed, 5 skipped.

Round 4 (gpt-5-4): VERIFIED, 0 findings. All four rounds ran through the S1 verify_session CLI (live dogfood). Cumulative verification cost $1.29. Cross-round ledger: V1 registry-resolution, V2 untracked-test-file, V4 manual-verify-vocabulary, V5 force-method-record all FIXED with regression tests; V3 resolved-by-process-order (close follows verification by construction).
Routed next-orchestrator analysis for Session 3 (gemini-pro, $0.005): Codex CLI gpt-5.4 @ medium (release integrity dictates; split-orchestrator option noted). ai-assignment.md S2 actuals filled.
